trivia_bullets.text: Saprazzan Skerry is from Mercadian Masques (October 4, 1999), the first set of the Masques block, set on the plane of Mercadia. 'Saprazzan' references Saprazzo — the coral city of Mercadia, a half-submerged merfolk metropolis inside a volcanic caldera lake, ruled by a Grand Vizier. Saprazzo is described as a rival trade city to the merchant-dominated Rishada, populated by merfolk with the ability to shift between fish-tail and humanoid form. The land art depicts a literal skerry (small rocky island) consistent with that coastal setting. [WebSearch via mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Saprazzo]

Saprazzan Skerry is one of a cycle of ten depletion lands from Mercadian Masques. Each enters with two depletion counters and produces double mana per activation, self-sacrificing when counters run out. The mechanic creates a deliberate front-loaded mana burst useful for explosive early turns but unsustainable. [Scryfall oracle text]

Saprazzan Skerry has never been reprinted outside Mercadian Masques (Scryfall: reprint false). It is not on the Reserved List. Foil scarcity from the old-frame 1999 print run drives the foil price to $47.36 versus $2.71 non-foil (as of cataloging). [Scryfall: reprint field, prices]
Frontmatter note: collector_number was blank at intake — corrected to 328 in this trivia pass via Scryfall verification (set mmq). [Scryfall]

Legal in Legacy, Vintage, Pauper, and Commander. EDHREC rank 6,531 — sees occasional Commander play as a blue mana accelerant in decks needing early blue mana density. In Legacy Pauper, double-blue lands are niche accelerants in storm or bounce-based combo shells. [Scryfall legalities; EDHREC rank via Scryfall]
